steps.title: Clarify the goal
steps.content: The audience already knows who Anjali Mehta is, so the introduction must focus on what makes The Holder of the World distinctive as a novel — not on biographical facts about the author. The correct choice will name the novel and describe its specific content.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — Anjali Mehta's settings include both twentieth-century United States and seventeenth-century India.: Describes settings in general and never even mentions the novel by name. ✗
  • Choice B — In addition to her novel The Holder of the World, which was published in 1993, Indian-born author Anjali Mehta wrote other novels and short stories.: Focuses on the author's wider career rather than introducing what the novel is about. ✗
  • Choice C — Anjali Mehta's novel The Holder of the World centers around two women, one living in twentieth-century United States and the other in seventeenth-century India.: Names the novel and describes its distinctive content — exactly what an audience familiar with Mehta needs. ✓
  • Choice D — The Holder of the World was not the only novel written by Indian-born author Anjali Mehta.: Provides introductory biographical context rather than describing the novel itself. ✗
That matches Choice C — Anjali Mehta's novel The Holder of the World centers around two women, one living in twentieth-century United States and the other in seventeenth-century India..
